qText: Statements : No lines are squares. Some squares are triangles. Conclusions : I. Some triangles are not lines. II. No triangles are lines.

Read the following statements and evaluate the conclusions based on traditional syllogistic logic. Consider the statements to be absolutely true, even if they appear contrary to known facts. Determine which conclusion(s) logically follow from the given Statements : All apples are fruits. Some fruits are sweet. Conclusions : I. Some apples are sweet. II. All sweet being fruits is a possibility.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : Some animals are omnivores. Some omnivores are wild. Conclusions : I. Some animals are wild. II. All wild are animals.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All trees are living beings. Some living beings are humans. Conclusions : I. Some trees are humans. II. All humans are trees.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All triangles are shapes. Some shapes are rectangles. All rectangles are polygons. Conclusions: I. Some shapes are polygons. II. Some polygons are triangles.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : No musicians are engineers. Some artists are engineers. Some musicians are artists. Conclusions : I. Some musicians are engineers. II. Some engineers are not musicians.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All fruits are healthy. All apples are fruits. Conclusions: I. All apples are healthy. II. All healthy are fruits.

Read the following statements carefully and identify the conclusions that logically follow from them. Statements: Some students are players. All players are disciplined. No disciplined person is lazy. Conclusions: I. Some students are disciplined. II. No player is lazy.
Statements: All doctors are educated. No educated person is ill-mannered. Conclusions: I. No doctor is ill-mannered. II. Some ill-mannered persons are doctors.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All apples are fruits. No fruit is green. All greens are vegetables. Conclusions: I. No apples are vegetables. II. No apples are green.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statements : All pencils are erasers. Some erasers are sharp. All sharp objects are pointed. Conclusions : I. Some erasers are pointed. II. Some pencils are pointed.
Statements: No table is a chair. Some chairs are wood. Conclusions: I. Some wood is not table. II. Some chairs are not table.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All teachers are educated. Some educated are doctors. All doctors are respected. Conclusions: I. Some teachers are respected. II. Some educated are respected.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some pens are paper. All paper are stationary. No stationary is electronic. Conclusions: I. Some pens are not electronic. II. Some stationary are pens.
Statements: Some singers are dancers. Some dancers are actors. Conclusions: I. Some singers are actors. II. All dancers being singers is a possibility.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All boys are students. Some students are boxer. No boxer is a scholar. Conclusions: I. Some boys may be boxer. II. No boys is a scholar.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All cars are vehicles. Some vehicles are cycles. No cycle is a car. Conclusions: I. Some cycles are cars. II. Some vehicles are not cars.
Statements: All pencils are erasers. Some erasers are boxes. Conclusions: I. Some boxes are pencils. II. All pencils are boxes.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All fruits are sweet. Some sweet things are healthy. No healthy thing is junk food. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are healthy. II. Some sweet things are not junk food.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: No cow are Buffalo. Some Buffalo are pets. All pets are friendly. Conclusions: I. Some pets are not cow. II. All buffalo are friendly.
Statements: Some Cars are Buses. All buses are Rickshaws. Conclusions: I. All Cars are buses. II. Some Rickshaws are buses .

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All chairs are tables. Some tables are desks. Conclusions: I. Some chairs are desks. II. All desks are tables.
Statements: Some balls are Bat. All Bat are Stamp. Conclusions: I. Some balls are Stamp. II. All stamps are balls.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All teachers are educated. Some educated people are scientists. Conclusions : I. Some scientists are teachers. II. Some teachers are educated.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All dogs are animals. Some animals are wild. No wild animal is domestic. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are not domestic. II. All animals are dogs.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some singers are artists. All artists are creative. Conclusions: I. Some singers are creative. II. All singers are creative.
Statements: Some books are pens. Some pens are pencils. Conclusions: I. Some pencils are books. II. All pens being books is a possibility.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: No chairs are tables. All tables are wooden. Conclusions: I. Some wooden things are not chairs. II. No chairs are wooden.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All teacher are intelligent. No intelligent is poor. Some poor are lazy. Conclusions: I. Some teacher are lazy. II. No intelligent is poor.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All roses are flowers. All flowers are plants. Conclusions: I. All roses are plants. II. Some plants are flowers.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: No student is careless. Some teachers are careless. Conclusions: I. No teacher is a student. II. Some careless people are teachers.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All fruits are nutritious. All nutritious is healthy. Conclusions: I. All fruits are healthy II. Some healthy is nutritious.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All students are hardworking. Some hardworking are successful. No successful is lazy. Conclusions : I. Some hardworking are not lazy. II. Some students are not lazy.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some students are intelligent. All intelligent people are hardworking. Conclusions: I. Some students are hardworking. II. All students are hardworking.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All pencils are pens. Some pens are markers. No marker is a book. Conclusions: I. Some pencils are not books. II. All markers are pens.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All cats are animals. Some animals are mammals. Conclusions: I. Some cats are mammals. II. All mammals are animals.
Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : Some A are B. All B are C. No C is D. Conclusions : I. Some A are C. II. Some B are not D.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All Doctors are skilled. No skilled person is allowed. Conclusions: I. All Doctors are allowed. II. Some skilled are allowed.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All books are paper. Some papers are red. No red is wood. Conclusions: I. Some books are not wood. II. All wood are paper.
Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: No mango is red. All cherries are mangoes. Conclusions: I. No cherry is red. II. Some mangoes are cherries.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: All books are notebooks. Some notebooks are not diaries. No diary is a ruler. Conclusions: I. Some books are not rulers. II. All rulers are diaries.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some flowers are red. All red things are beautiful. Conclusions: I. Some flowers are beautiful. II. All flowers are beautiful.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some books are pens. No pen is a pencil. Conclusions: I. Some books are not pencils. II. All pencils are pens.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement :- All teachers are readers. Some readers are learners. All learners are students. Conclusions :- I. Some teachers are students. II. Some readers are students.
Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : Some cat are wild. All wild are scary. Conclusions : I. Some cat are scary. II. All wild are cats.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement : All trees are living beings. Some living beings are humans. Conclusions : I. Some trees are humans. II. All humans are trees.
